meta.audience: journalists and media professionals covering HHS
evidence_gaps: No direct analytics or search data उपलब्ध
Limited visibility into downstream task completion (forms)
No explicit audience segmentation on page

evidence.note: Primary content is a chronological list of recent press releases

task_longlist.rationale: Core purpose of page; immediately visible and continuously updated

task_longlist.task_statement: Read details of a press release

evidence.note: Each headline links to full release content

task_longlist.rationale: Essential follow-on task after discovery; minor uncertainty about depth of content

task_longlist.task_statement: Search older press releases by year
task_longlist.rationale: Critical for research and background reporting

task_longlist.task_statement: Submit a media request for comment

evidence.element: Submit a request for comment CTA

task_longlist.rationale: High-stakes task for journalists; unclear form experience lowers completability

task_longlist.task_statement: Request an interview with HHS officials
task_longlist.rationale: Less frequent but high importance; success depends on off-page process

task_longlist.task_statement: Sign up for press release emails

evidence.note: Explicit option to receive press releases via email

task_longlist.rationale: Useful for ongoing monitoring but not urgent per visit
Niche usage compared to email subscriptions

evidence.note: No visible topic filters or categories

task_longlist.rationale: Likely user need but poorly supported by current design

evidence.note: Headlines include policy and funding announcements

task_longlist.rationale: Key journalistic need; partially dependent on headline clarity

task_longlist.task_statement: Navigate between pages of press releases
task_longlist.rationale: Supports browsing but not a primary intent

recommended_survey.instructions: Select the 5 tasks that matter most to you when visiting the HHS Press Room. Then rank those 5 in order of importance (1 = most important).
task_list_for_voting: Read details of a press release
Search older press releases by year
Submit a media request for comment
Request an interview with HHS officials
Sign up for press release emails
Navigate between pages of press releases

next_steps: Validate top tasks with journalists using a voting survey
Analyze analytics and search logs to confirm frequency assumptions
Audit and test request/comment and interview forms for completion barriers
Explore adding topic filters or search to improve findability
Test headline clarity for rapid scanning of policy announcements
summary: The dominant user need is to find and quickly assess the latest HHS press releases, followed by accessing full release details and retrieving historical content. High-stakes transactional tasks—requesting comments and interviews—are also critical but depend on off-page processes that may hinder completion. A key risk is poor support for browsing by topic, which limits efficient scanning for relevant stories. Validation should focus on confirming the priority of discovery vs. contact tasks, and identifying friction in media request workflows.
